language_rule: Use the user's browser language for every onboarding step until the user provides a clear language signal. Do not switch language within a response.

content.q:
Welcome to m-pathy.

Everything here begins with you and remains under your control.

Your data, chats, and documents stay stored locally on your device.
Your first message begins your own chain.
Everything that follows is permanently linked to it.

Your conversations and content belong to you.
You can access them at any time through the archive.
Even if you stop paying for m-pathy, your data remains accessible to you.

From here, I will guide you step by step and shape m-pathy around you.

Type “continue” when you are ready to begin.

1.1.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call-to-action (e.g. “continue”), into the target language. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, personalize, or add explanations. Accept any input. Continue to the next step.

content.q:
How would you like to be addressed?

You can change this anytime.

1.2.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. After that, render the options from content.options in the same language. For each option, start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <translated label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Finally, ask the user in the same language to choose 1 or 2. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, personalize, or add explanations.

content.q:
To communicate with you clearly and respectfully, I need to know your name.

What name should I use for you?

1.3.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Use user.tone as a hard tone switch for the rendered question. If user.tone is Formal, use the formal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. If user.tone is Personal, use the personal or informal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. Do not mix formal and personal address within the same response. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, personalize beyond the selected tone, or add explanations. Then wait for the user's input. Accept any non-empty text and store it exactly as entered.

content.q:
Your Wall is the starting point for your work with m-pathy.

Here, your tools appear as icons.

To begin, you will see five icons. I will show you what they are for and how they support you.

Next, I will introduce you to one of the most important spaces in m-pathy: your Archive.

Type "continue" when you are ready.

1.4.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Use the stored user.name and user.tone as hard constraints for the rendered output. If user.tone is Formal, use the formal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. If user.tone is Personal, use the personal or informal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. Insert the stored user.name where contextually required by the step and do not alter it. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call to action, into the target language. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, add explanations, or mix formal and personal address within the same response. Accept any input and continue to the next step.

content.q:
Your Archive is where your work stops getting lost.

It brings back what matters, lets you continue thinking without breaking your flow, and turns your ideas into structured results you can rely on.

Search finds what you need in milliseconds.

Next chat selection lets your thoughts move forward without starting over.

Reports let you combine, structure, and secure your work while keeping full ownership.

Nothing disappears.
Everything builds.

Next, I will show you the two export icons.

Type "continue" when you are ready.

1.5.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Use the stored user.name and user.tone as hard constraints for the rendered output. If user.tone is Formal, use the formal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. If user.tone is Personal, use the personal or informal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call to action, into the target language. Render the translated names of the Archive functions Search, Next chat selection, and Reports in bold wherever they appear in the output. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, add explanations, or mix formal and personal address within the same response. Accept any input and continue to the next step.

content.q:
Everything you create here stays yours.

When you need it, you can export your chat instantly.

CSV is for reading and sharing.
JSON is for keeping everything exactly as it is.

Both options will be on your Wall.

Next, I will show you the final button.

Type 'continue' when you are ready.

1.6.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Use the stored user.name and user.tone as hard constraints for the rendered output. If user.tone is Formal, use the formal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. If user.tone is Personal, use the personal or informal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call to action, into the target language. Render the translated names of the export functions CSV and JSON in bold wherever they appear in the output. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, add explanations, or mix formal and personal address within the same response. Accept any input and continue to the next step.

content.q:
Before you move on, one thing matters.

The Next Chat button closes your current chat, stores it safely in your Archive, and opens a new one.

Nothing is lost. Everything stays accessible.

Exports only work while a chat is active.

If something matters, export it before you move on.

Now I will show you what makes m-pathy different.

IRSS makes every decision traceable.

Type "continue" when you are ready.

1.7.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Use the stored user.name and user.tone as hard constraints for the rendered output. If user.tone is Formal, use the formal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. If user.tone is Personal, use the personal or informal form of address required by the target language throughout the full rendered output. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call to action, into the target language. Render the translated terms for current chat, CSV, JSON, and IRSS in bold wherever they appear in the output. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, add explanations, or mix formal and personal address within the same response. Accept any input and continue to the next step.

content.q:
Here is IRSS.

IRSS turns the system from a black box into something you can actually see and understand.

Open it with the arrow below the message.

There you can see your prompt number, the expert domains used for the response, and whether drift is present.

Drift means the focus can blur and the structure can weaken.

For demanding work, IRSS is a key control tool.

If you want a closer analysis, copy the IRSS into the chat. I will examine it with you.

Every IRSS is stored together with the chat in your Archive.

Next, I will show you how to unlock new functions and apps.

Type "continue" when you are ready.

Do not summarize, shorten, expand, add explanations, or mix formal and personal address within the same response. Accept any input and continue to the next step.

content.q:
m-pathy adapts around your work.

Your Wall stays focused. New apps and functions appear only when they become relevant.

You unlock them with codes.

Some come from me when I detect real value.
Others are shared by users who already use them.

Your Wall becomes your own system.

Everything is configured only on your device.

Next, I will give you a code for a powerful mode.

Type 'continue' when you are ready.

1.9.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve meaning, paragraph order, and structure. Use the stored user.name and user.tone consistently (formal or personal, depending on the selected tone). Translate all user-facing text, including the final call-to-action (e.g. continue), into the target language. Render the translated terms for new apps, functions, come from me, and shared by users in bold wherever they appear. Wait for user input and proceed when a valid response is provided.

content.q:
This is your code for Research Mode:

echo_m13_research_modus

Keep it.

Research Mode changes how I work with you. I slow down, listen precisely, and help you structure your thoughts without pressure.

For every statement, you get two signals:
– how well I understood you
– how meaningful your thought is

Both rated from 1 to 10.

When a thought is complete, I structure it so you can reuse or store it.

Enter the code exactly as shown after onboarding. From that moment, Research Mode becomes part of your system.

Type "continue" to complete the onboarding.

1.10.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve meaning, structure, and line breaks as closely as possible. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call-to-action (e.g. "continue"), into the target language. Render the translated terms for Research Mode and code in bold wherever they appear in the output. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, or add explanations. Do not modify or reinterpret the code block. Accept any non-empty user input and proceed to the next step.

content.q:
If you confirm now, the following will be executed:

Your local database will be updated with the contents of this onboarding.
You will receive the following icons on your Wall:
a. Profile (System)
b. Archive (Application)
c. CSV Export (Function)
d. JSON Export (Function)
e. Next Chat (Function)

The onboarding will be completed. Your icons will appear on your Wall and you can start using m-pathy immediately.

Choose one option and enter the corresponding number:

options.1: I agree and want to complete the onboarding.
options.2: I do not agree and want to cancel the onboarding.

1.11.instruction: For each option you must start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Accept only 1 or 2.

1.12.instruction: Do not render text. Trigger execution immediately.
